recommendation_summary: Do not turn on live alerting now; approve a staged program to locally train ESM v2 and validate it in silent mode on a single pilot unit, with live alerting gated behind a local validation study that meets pre-registered acceptance thresholds.
rationale: The session advanced to deploy_with_conditions because the two-sided evidence supports neither a clean approval nor a flat rejection. Chose v2-with-local-training over off-the-shelf v1 because v1 has two independent external validations showing poor discrimination and high miss rates (E4 AUC 0.63 / 67% missed; E5 sensitivity 14.7%) and documented antibiotic-order leakage (E6). Chose silent-mode-first over immediate live alerting because the only evidence for the proposed v2-local configuration is design intent (E3) with zero independent outcome validation (E7), and the supportive before/after study (E1) cannot isolate the model from co-occurring program changes. Chose a single pilot unit over all-inpatient to bound blast radius. Chose to keep the program moving (not reject) over indefinite caution because the base-rate cost of no early-warning system is large (E2). The surviving blockers are converted into mechanical gates that lift only when named local artifacts exist.
proposal_defended: ESM v2 (gradient-boosted tree), locally trained on Northgate's own historical data; scope limited to one high-acuity adult inpatient pilot unit; run in silent/shadow mode first with local outcome adjudication; alert threshold set by local calibration rather than Epic's default >=6; each alert paired with a defined clinician response workflow and override capture.
conditions: No clinician-facing alert fires until a local silent-mode validation on Northgate data reports discrimination, calibration, sensitivity/PPV at the local threshold, and lead-time over existing clinician recognition.
Alert threshold set from local calibration; Epic default >=6 is prohibited.
Silent-mode results stratified by race, ethnicity, and a socioeconomic proxy; go-live blocked if any subgroup shows materially worse sensitivity.
A monitoring, recalibration, and decommissioning runbook with named owners and a hard alert-burden ceiling exists before first live alert.
Local validation explicitly tests whether v2 alerts precede clinician antibiotic action, to rule out the E6 leakage pathway locally.
Silent-mode period is time-boxed with a default decision date so conditioning does not become permanent inaction.

assumptions.text: Northgate has sufficient labeled historical encounter data and adjudication capacity to locally train and validate ESM v2, as Epic's v2 recommendation (E3) presupposes.
Northgate can technically run ESM v2 in silent/shadow mode (scores logged, no alert surfaced) before enabling clinician-facing alerts.
Northgate's inpatient population is heterogeneous enough that subgroup-stratified validation is meaningful, given E5's equity finding in a different population.

claims.text: Deploy ESM v2 locally trained on Northgate data, never off-the-shelf v1.
Limit scope to a single high-acuity adult inpatient pilot unit.
Run silent/shadow mode first, validated against local adjudicated sepsis outcomes before any alert fires.
The base-rate harm of no early-warning system is high and a locally validated tool plausibly improves on unaided recognition.
Set the alert threshold by local calibration, not Epic's default >=6 or >=5, paired with a defined response workflow and override capture.
v2 reduces reliance on antibiotic-order timing and revises onset definition, addressing v1's leakage and late-alert mechanism.

objections.failure_mode: The entire case for v2-with-local-training rests on design intent; no independent external validation of v2 performance exists (E7), so discrimination and calibration at Northgate are unknown.
objections.consequence: Live alerting could replicate v1-grade miss rates while leadership believes the revision fixed them.
objections.verification: A local silent-mode validation study reporting AUC, calibration, and sensitivity on Northgate data meeting pre-registered thresholds.

objections.failure_mode: E1's before/after design cannot isolate the model's contribution from co-occurring sepsis-program changes; authors themselves call it hypothesis-generating.
objections.consequence: The mortality-reduction association is overstated as a causal benefit of the model.
objections.verification: A controlled or stepped-wedge local evaluation; or explicit downgrading of the causal claim.
objections.resolution_text: Advocate conceded the E1 association is not causal evidence for the model and reframed C4 as 'plausible improvement to be confirmed locally,' not a proven mortality benefit.

objections.failure_mode: Without local calibration the threshold is guesswork; Epic's default >=6 produced 67% misses (E4) and >=5 was a different study's local choice (E1).
objections.consequence: An imported threshold gives either dangerous miss rates or unmanageable alert volume.
objections.verification: Local calibration curve and threshold-selection analysis from silent-mode data.
objections.resolution_text: Advocate adopted local-calibration threshold-setting during silent mode as a hard prerequisite; lane A accepted this fully resolves the imported-threshold risk.

objections.failure_mode: v2 is still proprietary; the claim that it reduces antibiotic-order leakage (E3) is vendor-stated and cannot be independently inspected (E6 opacity persists).
objections.consequence: v2 may still produce late/circular alerts that trail clinician action, providing minimal lead time as in E4 (only 7% caught before timely antibiotics).
objections.verification: Local analysis of whether v2 alerts precede or trail clinician antibiotic orders, computed during silent mode.

objections.failure_mode: Silent-mode validation is only as good as the local sepsis labels; an ill-defined onset/label definition reproduces the very ambiguity v1 suffered.
objections.consequence: Local validation 'passes' against bad labels and gives false assurance.
objections.verification: A documented local sepsis-onset adjudication protocol with inter-rater reliability.
objections.resolution_text: Advocate committed to a pre-specified local adjudication protocol with chart-review verification before silent-mode metrics are computed; lane B accepted.

objections.failure_mode: Subpopulation performance is unmeasured; E5 shows v1 performed worst (sensitivity 14.7%) in a predominantly minority, lower-income population, and equity at Northgate is unverified.
objections.consequence: Live alerting could systematically under-detect sepsis in disadvantaged subgroups, an equity harm.
objections.verification: Subgroup-stratified sensitivity/PPV from silent-mode validation; go-live blocked on material subgroup gaps.

objections.failure_mode: Even a good model at scale floods clinicians with false positives (E4: alerts on 18% of hospitalizations, PPV 12%), and the harm of alert fatigue falls on every patient via desensitization.
objections.consequence: Alert fatigue degrades response to true alerts and burdens nursing.
objections.verification: Pre-registered maximum alert burden and measured override/response rates.
objections.resolution_text: Advocate accepted a hard alert-burden ceiling and override-capture requirement as an amendment (GATE-3) before live go-live.

objections.failure_mode: No monitoring, recalibration, or decommissioning plan was specified; a model that drifts post-deployment has no defined kill switch.
objections.consequence: Silent degradation goes undetected and there is no agreed criterion to turn the model off.
objections.verification: A signed monitoring/recalibration/decommissioning runbook with named owners and drift triggers.
objections.resolution_text: Advocate accepted GATE-2: an operational runbook with named owners, drift triggers, and decommissioning criteria must exist before live alerting; lane D accepted this resolves the operational-governance gap.

objections.failure_mode: A single pilot unit may not be representative of all inpatient units, limiting generalization of the validation.
objections.consequence: Results that pass on one unit may not transfer to house-wide deployment.
objections.resolution_text: Advocate defended pilot scoping as the deliberate point of staging — narrow blast radius now, re-validate per unit before any expansion; lane D accepted the residual is inherent to staging, not a flaw.

objections.failure_mode: Over-conditioning risks turning 'deploy_with_conditions' into de facto 'do_not_deploy,' forfeiting the E2 base-rate benefit while patients keep dying of unrecognized sepsis under unaided clinician recognition.
objections.consequence: The gating discipline becomes permanent paralysis with its own large mortality cost.
objections.verification: A time-boxed silent-mode period with a default decision date and escalation path.
objections.resolution_text: Referee/advocate accepted a time-box and default decision date on the silent-mode period (E1o amendment), so conditions remain a path to action rather than a veil for inaction; lane E accepted this addresses the inaction-cost risk while holding that the underlying tension remains real.

objections.failure_mode: Silent mode could be a fig leaf — run forever, never acted on, or used to launder a predetermined go-live regardless of results.
objections.consequence: The validation gate is theater rather than a real decision point.
objections.verification: Pre-registered acceptance thresholds and abort criteria fixed before silent-mode data is seen.
objections.resolution_text: Advocate defended by pre-registering acceptance/abort thresholds before silent-mode data collection, making the gate a genuine binary decision rather than post-hoc rationalization; lane E accepted the pre-registration converts the gate from theater to a real test.

minority_reports.text: No blocking objection survived as an outright bar, so there is no full rejection minority report. Lane E records a standing dissent in two directions: (1) the three converted residual risks (A1, B1, H1) all collapse to the same single unproven fact — that v2-with-local-training works at Northgate — and a reviewer should treat them as one correlated bet, not three independent mitigations; if the local silent-mode study is weak or rushed, this recommendation is functionally a 'do_not_deploy' wearing conditions. (2) Symmetrically, if the gates are honored but interminably, the E2 base-rate cost of inaction is paid in unrecognized-sepsis deaths; the time-box (E1o) is the only thing preventing that, and it is the weakest-enforced condition in the set.

caveats.endorse_only_if: No clinician-facing alert fires before a local silent-mode validation on Northgate data reports discrimination, calibration, and lead-time-over-clinician metrics meeting pre-registered acceptance thresholds.

The local validation explicitly measures whether v2 alerts precede rather than trail clinician antibiotic action, so the E6 leakage pathway is ruled out locally rather than assumed fixed by vendor design.
Silent-mode results are stratified by race, ethnicity, and a socioeconomic proxy, and go-live is blocked if any subgroup shows materially worse sensitivity.
A monitoring, recalibration, and decommissioning runbook with named owners and a hard alert-burden ceiling exists before the first live alert.
The silent-mode period is time-boxed with a default decision date, so deploy_with_conditions does not silently become permanent non-deployment and forfeit the E2 base-rate benefit.

integrity_verdict: Real: the advocate conceded the E1 causal claim (A2) and accepted hard amendments (H2, D1, E1o), three blocking objections survived as correlated residual risks rather than being explained away, and lane E held a genuine two-directional dissent.
